message:
feat(ft-xbnl0.2.3): cx-first ft record poll + audit stream follow (tick 290)

3 ambient async sites migrated across 2 CLI surfaces in main.rs:

**`ft audit stream --follow` poll sleep (L28314):**

- `runtime_compat::sleep(Duration::from_millis(poll_interval_ms))` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&storage_cx_stream, Duration::from_millis(poll_interval_ms))`
  with Err-handling that breaks the follow loop on cx-cancel. Reuses
  the existing tick-271 `storage_cx_stream` binding from the
  `get_audit_actions_stream_with_cx` call above.

**`ft record` pane-capture poll loop (L30073 + L30104):**

- `wez.get_text(pane_id, false)` →
  `get_text_with_cx(&record_cx, pane_id, false)` — the pane-capture
  RPC now propagates cx-cancel.
- `runtime_compat::sleep(Duration::from_millis(200))` →
  `sleep_with_cx(&record_cx, Duration::from_millis(200))` with
  Err-handling that breaks the record loop on cx-cancel. Shared
  `record_cx` binding declared above the loop.

The `ft record` poll loop can run for an unbounded duration (recording
sessions can be hours). Cx-cancel (e.g., Ctrl-C) now aborts the loop
at the next poll boundary without waiting for the current 200ms slice
or pending `get_text` RPC.

Verification: `cargo check -p frankenterm --bin ft` compiles clean.

Cumulative ft-xbnl0.2.3 ticks this session: 104 (187-290).
main.rs cross-crate migration total: 191 sites (188 before + 3).
frankenterm-core internal sites: 47 (unchanged this tick).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
